mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, tight labor market conditions trigger wage growth pressures that compress corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as earnings revisions turn adverse.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous surge in industrial production and residential housing starts signals robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, which historically precedes positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate revenues accelerate.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or the combined Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, a contraction in real consumer credit signals tightening financial conditions that restrict household spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as consumer discretionary earnings face headwinds.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 50% of triggered episodes, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and corporate hiring, which supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations, or the rolling 1-year Rank IC consistently falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth exceeds 3.0%, the economy operates above potential, driving inflation expectations higher and prompting equity markets to price in future monetary tightening, which suppresses forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally with positive 20d returns in > 60% of high-GDP-growth episodes, contradicting the tightening expectation channel.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising unemployment and stagnant industrial production indicates weakening labor demand alongside softening output, which historically triggers defensive equity positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, a sharp deceleration in consumer credit growth signals demand destruction, which reduces corporate revenue growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect shows no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward testing.
